text: When building software, testing plays a huge role. Two common types are unit tests and integration tests. Unit tests check small parts of code, like individual functions, to ensure they work correctly on their own. Integration tests verify that different parts of the system work together smoothly. Many developers use test-driven development, where they write tests first and then write the code to pass them. This process leads to cleaner, more reliable software. Overall, thorough testing gives developers confidence. They can make changes and add new features knowing that their tests will catch mistakes before the software reaches users.
